description: Wire the spec's delivery-link feature: task files carry a delivered_in field pointing at the squash commit on main. Ground truth is the [bl-xxxx] tag embedded in the commit message, discoverable via git log --grep. The SHA is a performance hint; on read, balls verifies the SHA still exists and still contains the tag, and re-resolves via tag scan if not. Handles rebase/amend/filter-branch gracefully. Bl show <id> gains the ability to display and open the delivering commit. Bl review writes the hint when the squash commit lands. Tests: rebase then show still resolves; deleted SHA re-resolves via tag; task never reviewed has no delivery link. Reference docs/SPEC-orphan-branch-state.md §6. Depends on topology task being merged (it is).
title: SPEC §6: delivery-tag resolution + self-healing SHA cache
